# TenancyJS

> TenancyJS is a fail-closed, TypeScript-first multi-tenancy toolkit for Node.js. It gives every
> framework and ORM the same tenant-context and isolation contract — tenant identity follows the async
> execution scope, and any tenant-aware data access without a valid context throws instead of returning
> another tenant's data. No hosted service, no lock-in: bring your own store, framework, and ORM.

TenancyJS is a monorepo of small, composable `tenancyjs-*` packages: a framework-neutral core, adapters
that enforce isolation inside your ORM, framework integrations that bind tenant context to the request
lifecycle, and an operational CLI. It supports three isolation strategies behind one contract —
single-database row-level (forced Postgres RLS or query-scoping), schema-per-tenant (transaction-local
`search_path`), and database-per-tenant (a bounded, cache-routed connection per tenant).

Key principles for an agent reading this project:

- **Fail closed.** Tenant identity rides `AsyncLocalStorage`. A tenant-aware read/write with no valid
  context throws — there is never a silent fallback to unscoped data.
- **Honesty over faith.** A capability flips to `"supported"` ONLY after a real two-tenant adversarial
  isolation test on a real database. Never mark something supported without that evidence; the CLI's
  `tenant check` surfaces untested adapter/strategy combos as warnings.
- **Repository memory in `docs/` is the source of truth**, above chat history. Never contradict an
  accepted ADR in `docs/adrs/` — supersede it through the documented process instead.
- **Unopinionated boundaries.** The registry is bring-your-own (`TenantStore`), hardened at the boundary
  so a buggy store cannot return the wrong tenant. The CLI is ORM-agnostic: provisioning and migrations
  delegate to host-provided hooks — the CLI never invokes an ORM itself.
- **Tenant identity is not authorization.** Your app still owns auth; TenancyJS owns isolation.

## Start here

- [README](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Karthick-Ramachandran/TenancyJS/main/README.md): What it is, install, the three strategies, supported stacks, and the operational CLI.
- [Philosophy](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Karthick-Ramachandran/TenancyJS/main/PHILOSOPHY.md): Why it exists and the deliberate bet on fail-closed isolation and tested honesty.
- [Product PRD](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Karthick-Ramachandran/TenancyJS/main/docs/00-product/PRD.md): The problem, users, scope, and non-goals.
- [Conventions](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Karthick-Ramachandran/TenancyJS/main/docs/60-engineering/CONVENTIONS.md): The canonical components, helpers, and patterns to reuse (never reinvent).

## Architecture

- [Architecture](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Karthick-Ramachandran/TenancyJS/main/docs/10-architecture/ARCHITECTURE.md): Core, adapters, integrations, and how tenant context flows.
- [ADR-0017: Isolation strategy model and routing contract](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Karthick-Ramachandran/TenancyJS/main/docs/adrs/ADR-0017-tenant-isolation-strategy-model-and-routing-contract.md)
- [ADR-0018: Schema-per-tenant enforcement (search_path + optional per-tenant role)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Karthick-Ramachandran/TenancyJS/main/docs/adrs/ADR-0018-schema-per-tenant-enforcement-search-path-with-optional-per-tenant-role.md)
- [ADR-0019: Shared Postgres strategy engine](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Karthick-Ramachandran/TenancyJS/main/docs/adrs/ADR-0019-adapter-shared-postgres-strategy-engine.md)
- [ADR-0022: Database-per-tenant, cache-routed connections](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Karthick-Ramachandran/TenancyJS/main/docs/adrs/ADR-0022-database-per-tenant-strategy-cache-routed-per-tenant-connections.md)

## Operational CLI

- [ADR-0028: Tenant store contract (bring-your-own, hardened)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Karthick-Ramachandran/TenancyJS/main/docs/adrs/ADR-0028-tenant-store-contract-for-the-operational-cli.md)
- [ADR-0029: CLI architecture — config loading, command engine, provisioning via host hooks](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Karthick-Ramachandran/TenancyJS/main/docs/adrs/ADR-0029-cli-operational-architecture-config-loading-command-engine-and-provisioning-via.md)

## Security and quality

- [Security Model](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Karthick-Ramachandran/TenancyJS/main/docs/20-security/SECURITY_MODEL.md): The fail-closed guarantees and what is (and isn't) TenancyJS's job.
- [Security Policy](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Karthick-Ramachandran/TenancyJS/main/SECURITY.md): How to report a vulnerability.
- [Quality Gates](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Karthick-Ramachandran/TenancyJS/main/docs/50-quality/QUALITY_GATES.md): The full gate, coverage thresholds, and the adversarial-test requirement.
- [Lessons](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Karthick-Ramachandran/TenancyJS/main/docs/60-engineering/LESSONS.md): Non-obvious failures learned the hard way (e.g. why Prisma schema-per-tenant is unsupported).

## Contributing

- [Contributing](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Karthick-Ramachandran/TenancyJS/main/CONTRIBUTING.md): How to build, test against real databases, and land a change.
